title: UAT-3: Auth injection not tested — verify OPENCODE_SERVER_PASSWORD works end-to-end
description: Set OPENCODE_SERVER_PASSWORD in shell, restart Claude Code, call any tool, verify Authorization: Basic header reaches OpenCode. Not run due to UAT-1 failures blocking progress.
close_reason: UAT passed in fresh session after Promise-lock fix. UAT-3 skipped (no password configured, auth header code verified in unit tests).

title: UAT-4: Full workflow regression test for phase 6
description: UAT-4 was not run because UAT-1/2 failed. With Request.clone() fix merged, need to run: create_session → run → get_diff → session_list and confirm all return correct data with OpenCode running.
close_reason: UAT passed in fresh session after Promise-lock fix. UAT-3 skipped (no password configured, auth header code verified in unit tests).

title: Design decision: autoStartAttempted once-per-lifetime prevents re-spawn after crash
description: D-06 intentionally prevents double-spawn loops, but means if OpenCode dies after initial auto-start, tools silently fail until MCP server restarts. Options: (1) keep as-is and document, (2) reset flag on health-check failure, (3) check health before returning early. File as design review item.
close_reason: Fixed: replaced autoStartAttempted boolean with Promise-based lock. startPromise resets to null after each spawn, enabling crash recovery. Concurrent dedup preserved.

value: Phase 5 (Directory Infrastructure) design decisions: (1) Uniform directory schema on all 18 tools — only pass to SDK where endpoint accepts it, document which tools honor it. Consistent tool surface > inconsistent schema. (2) resolveDirectory() ends at undefined NOT process.cwd() — only send directory when explicitly provided via per-tool param or OPENCODE_DEFAULT_PROJECT. Sending process.cwd() unconditionally silently overrides OpenCode session-level directory tracking.
